This table contains records of various observations made about individuals. It includes social history factors, ethnicity background, pregnancy and delivery observations, and other observations that are not captured in other tables.


observation_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each observation in the observations table.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

The unique identifier for the person to whom the observation belongs.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('person')(person_id)




observation_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the standard concept in the Concept table representing the observation being made. This field is the primary id for analysis.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




observation_date (DATE)

The date when the observation was made, in the format YYYY-MM-DD. For ethnicity background, the date is the latest date from visit occurrence.



observation_datetime (DATETIME)

The exact date and time when the observation was recorded.



observation_type_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the standard concept in the Concept table that specifies the type of observation such as patient reported or EHR
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




value_as_number (FLOAT64)

The quantitative value of the observation, represented as a number (float).



value_as_string (STRING)

The qualitative value of the observation, represented as a string such as the categories for ethinicty



value_as_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the standard concept in the Concept table that represents the value of the observation.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




qualifier_concept_id (INT64)

Optional qualifier for the observation, providing additional granularity. Currently all null values



unit_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the standard concept in the Concept table representing the unit of measurement for the observation such as mL.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




provider_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the provider who made the observation, representing healthcare personnel involved.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('provider')(provider_id)




visit_occurrence_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier for the visit during which the observation was made
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('visit_occurrence')(visit_occurrence_id)




visit_detail_id (INT64)

A foreign key detailed identifier for the specific visit that generated the observation.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('visit_detail')(visit_detail_id)




observation_source_value (STRING)

The raw source value for the observation as provided in the source data.



observation_source_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier corresponding to the concept_id in the concept table for the observation’s source concept in the source data.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




unit_source_value (STRING)

The raw source value for the unit of measurement as provided in the source data.



qualifier_source_value (STRING)

The raw source value for the qualifier as provided in the source data, if applicable.



value_source_value (STRING)

The raw value of the observation from the source data. This field houses the verbatim result value of the Observation from the source data.



observation_event_id (INT64)

If the Observation record is related to another record in the database, this field is the primary key of the linked record.



obs_event_field_concept_id (INT64)

If the Observation record is related to another record in the database, this field is the CONCEPT_ID that identifies which table the primary key of the linked record came from.

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
OBSERVATION_PERIOD
The observation period table is intended to contain records of spans of time in which a patient’s clinical event history is considered observable, i.e. clinical events that happen to the person are recorded in the event tables, and absence of records indicate such events did not occur during this span of time. Please note that since STARR-OMOP only contains records of clinical events that are present in the Stanford EHR systems, it is not possible to determine if the absence of an event means that the event did not occur or if the patient was instead treated in a different healthcare system.


observation_period_id (INT64)

The unique key given to each OMOP observation period record. In STARR-OMOP, this ID is currently NOT stable across releases, so the same person’s observation period may have a different ID from one STARR-OMOP release to the next. Instead, use the person_id to uniquely identify a given observation period, as each person only has one observation period using our current algorithm for defining observation periods.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

Constraints:

Not Null




observation_period_start_date (DATE)

The date on which the observation period for the person begins. This is calculated as the date of the earliest clinical event recorded for the person in the OMOP CDM tables visit_occurrence, condition_occurrence, procedure_occurrence, measurement, drug_exposure, device_exposure, and observation.
Constraints:

Not Null




observation_period_end_date (DATE)

The date on which the observation period for the person ends. This is calculated as the earliest of the following three dates: 1) patient’s death date + 60 day grace period, 2) source data cut date, 3) date of the latest clinical event recorded for the person in the OMOP CDM tables visit_occurrence, condition_occurrence, procedure_occurrence, measurement, drug_exposure, device_exposure, and observation.
Constraints:

Not Null




period_type_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to a standard concept_id in the concept table for the type of observation period. In STARR-OMOP, this is set to 32882, ‘Standard algorithm from EHR’.
Constraints:

Not Null

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
PAYER_PLAN_PERIOD
This table contains information about the period of time that a Person is continuously enrolled under a specific health Plan benefit structure from a given Payer. Each Person receiving healthcare is typically covered by a health benefit plan, which fully/partially pays for (or directly provides) the care (billable healthcare services). These benefit plans are provided by payers, such as health insurances or state or government agencies. In each plan the details of the health benefits are defined for the Person or her family, and the health benefit Plan might change over time with increasing utilization (reaching certain cost thresholds such as deductibles), plan availability and purchasing choices of the Person. The unique combinations of Payer organizations, health benefit Plans and time periods in which they are valid for a Person (Payer Plan Periods) are recorded in this table.


payer_plan_period_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each record in the payer_plan_period table.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the person table for the person covered by the plan detailed in each payer_plan_period record.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('person')(person_id)




payer_plan_period_start_date (DATE)

Start date of the plan coverage described in each payer_plan_period record. This is obtained from the mem_eff_from_date in the STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables.



payer_plan_period_end_date (DATE)

End date of the plan coverage described in each payer_plan_period record. This is obtained from the mem_eff_to_date in the STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables.



payer_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the standard concept_id in the concept table. Upon mapping payer_concept_id to the standard concept_id in the concept table, this field represents the entity that would reimburse billable services for any provider(s) administering care to the person in each payer_plan_period record. It contains the standard concept obtained after mapping the qrda_sop_code_c field from STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables to the concept_code field in the concept table. The concept domain is ‘Payer’ and the vocabulary is ‘SOPT’.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




payer_source_value (STRING)

Payer information as it appears in the source data. The value is represented as an array containing the fin_class_name, qrda_sopt_code_c, and sop_code_name values from the STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables.



payer_source_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the source concept_id in the concept table. This field represents the entity that would reimburse billable services for any provider(s) administering care to the person in each payer_plan_period record, as it appears in the source data. It contains the source concept obtained after mapping the qrda_sop_code_c field from STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables to the concept_code field in the concept table. The concept domain is ‘Payer’ and the vocabulary is ‘SOPT’.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




plan_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the specific health benefit plan that the person in each payer_plan_period record is enrolled in. This is not currently populated in STARR OMOP due to the need for custom mapping.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




plan_source_value (STRING)

This field represents the specific health benefit plan that the person in each payer_plan_period record is enrolled in, as detailed in the source data. The value is represented as an array containing the benefit_plan_id and benefit_plan_name values from the STARR Common shc_payors and lpch_payors tables.



plan_source_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the specific health benefit plan that the person in each payer_plan_period record is enrolled in, as mapped from the source data. This is not currenlty populated in STARR OMOP due to the need for custom mapping.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




sponsor_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the sponsor who finances the plan detailed in each payer_plan_period record. This includes self-insured, small group health plan and large group health plan. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




sponsor_source_value (STRING)

This is the plan sponsor as it appears in the source data. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.



sponsor_source_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the sponsor of the plan as mapped from the source data. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




family_source_value (STRING)

The common identifier for all people (often a family) that covered by the same policy. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.



stop_reason_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the reason the person left the plan detailed in each payer_plan_period record, if known. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




stop_reason_source_value (STRING)

This is the plan stop reason as it appears in the source data. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.



stop_reason_source_concept_id (INT64)

This field represents the plan stop reason as mapped from the source data. We are not populating this in STARR OMOP currently.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
PERSON
This table serves as the central identity management for all Persons in the database. It contains records that uniquely identify each person or patient, and some demographic information. If the patient is present in both SHC and LPCH Clarity, the SHC patient record is used.


person_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each person in the person table.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




gender_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s sex. The naming of this field as ‘gender’ is outdated; it should not be used to study the patient’s gender identity.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




year_of_birth (INT64)

Year of birth of the person, in the format YYYY. The year of birth is extracted from the person’s date of birth.



month_of_birth (INT64)

Month of birth of the person, as an integer. The month of birth is extracted from the person’s date of birth.



day_of_birth (INT64)

Day of birth of the person, as an integer. The day of birth is extracted from the person’s date of birth.



birth_datetime (datetime)

Date and time of birth of the person. Time defaults to 00:00:00 if unavailable.



race_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s race. If multiple races are recorded in the source data for that patient, this field will be set to 0, as there is currently no standard concept for multiple races. See the race_source_value field to determine if the patient has multiple races.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




ethnicity_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s ethnicity (Hispanic/Latino or Non-Hispanic/Non-Latino). Additional ethnic background information is available in the observation table.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




location_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the location in the Location table representing the most recent known address for the patient.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('location')(location_id)




provider_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the provider in the Provider table representing the patient’s most recent primary care provider.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('provider')(provider_id)




care_site_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the care site in the Care Site table representing the patient’s most recent known care site.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('care_site')(care_site_id)




person_source_value (STRING)

A unique identifier for each person as it appears in the source data. In identified STARR-OMOP, this field contains the patient’s medical record number (MRN) and date of birth concatenated with a pipe, ex. “12345678 | 1900-01-01”.



gender_source_value (STRING)

The verbatim value for the patient’s sex as it appears in the source data. The naming of this field as ‘gender’ is outdated; it should not be used to study the patient’s gender identity.



gender_source_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s sex as it appears in the source data. The naming of this field as ‘gender’ is outdated; it should not be used to study the patient’s gender identity.



race_source_value (STRING)

The verbatim value for the patient’s race as it appears in the source data. If multiple races are recorded in the source data for that patient, then this field will say ‘Multiple’.



race_source_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s race as it appears in the source data.



ethnicity_source_value (STRING)

The verbatim value for the patient’s ethnicty (Hispanic/Latino or Non-Hispanic/Non-Latino) as it appears in the source data. Additional ethnic background information is available in the observation table.



ethnicity_source_concept_id (INT64)

Foreign key to the concept in the Concept table representing the patient’s ethnicity (Hispanic/Latino or Non-Hispanic/Non-Latino) as it appears in the source data.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE
The PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE table contains records of procedures that have been performed on patients. The procedures are typically performed by healthcare providers and are recorded in the source data. For STARR-OMOP this includes ordered procedures and billed procedures.


procedure_occurrence_id (INT64)

The unique key given to a procedure record for a person.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

The person_id of the person for whom the procedure is recorded. This can be used to join to the person table to find more information about the person
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('person')(person_id)




procedure_concept_id (INT64)

The PROCEDURE_CONCEPT_ID field is recommended for primary use in analyses, and must be used for network studies. This is the standard concept mapped from the source value which represents a procedure
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




procedure_date (DATE)

This is the date when the procedure was performed or ordered on the patient. This is the start date of the procedure.



procedure_datetime (DATETIME)

This is the date and time where the procedure was performed / ordered on the patient.



procedure_end_date (DATE)

This is the date where the procedure ended. If not available is set to the same as procedure_date.



procedure_end_datetime (DATETIME)

This is the date and time where the procedure ended. If not available is set to the same as procedure_datetime.



procedure_type_concept_id (INT64)

This field is used to determine the provenance of the Procedure record.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




modifier_concept_id (INT64)

The modifiers are intended to give additional information about the procedure. In some cases up to 4 modifiers can be associated with a procedure in the source data. In this column we are selecting only the first modifier from the source data.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




quantity (INT64)

This is the quantity of the procedure performed. If not available is set to 1.



provider_id (INT64)

The provider associated with the procedure record, e.g. the provider who ordered or performed the Procedure.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('provider')(provider_id)




visit_occurrence_id (INT64)

The visit during which the procedure occurred.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('visit_occurrence')(visit_occurrence_id)




visit_detail_id (INT64)

The VISIT_DETAIL record during which the Procedure occurred. For example, if the Person was in the ICU at the time of the Procedure the VISIT_OCCURRENCE record would reflect the overall hospital stay and the VISIT_DETAIL record would reflect the ICU stay during the hospital visit. Currently it is not populated for this table
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('visit_detail')(visit_detail_id)




procedure_source_value (STRING)

this is the source value as it appears in the source data. Includes a JSON string with the provenance of the data that includes names and codes.



procedure_source_concept_id (INT64)

This is the concept representing the procedure source value and may not necessarily be standard. This field is discouraged from use in analysis because it is not required to contain Standard Concepts that are used across the OHDSI community, and should only be used when Standard Concepts do not adequately represent the source detail for the Procedure necessary for a given analytic use case. Consider using PROCEDURE_CONCEPT_ID instead to enable standardized analytics that can be consistent across the network.



modifier_source_value (STRING)

This field houses the four possible modifiers found in the source data. It is a JSON string with the values of the four possible modifiers

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
PROVIDER
The PROVIDER table contains a list of uniquely identified healthcare providers. These are individuals providing hands-on healthcare to patients, such as physicians, nurses, midwives, physical therapists etc.


provider_id (int64)

This is the primary key of the table. This is a unique identifier for every provider. It is assumed that every provider with a different unique identifier is in fact a different person and should be treated independently.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




provider_name (string)

The name of the provider as it appears in the source data



npi (string)

This is the National Provider Number issued to health care providers in the US by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS).



dea (string)

This is the identifier issued by the DEA, a US federal agency, that allows a provider to write prescriptions for controlled substances.



specialty_concept_id (int64)

This is the standardized specialty associated with the provider. For providers with multiple specialties, the first specialty that is listed in Clarity is selected. This field includes physician specialties such as internal medicine, emergency medicine, etc. and allied health professionals such as nurses, midwives, and pharmacists.
Constraints:

Not Null




care_site_id (int64)

This is the CARE_SITE_ID for the location that the provider primarily practices in.



year_of_birth (int64)

Year of birth of the provider



gender_concept_id (int64)

This is the standardized representation for the provider’s sex. 8532 for Female, 8507 for Male.



provider_source_value (string)

This field contains a combination of the Clarity unique provider identifier and the source data (shc, lpch) (e.g. “12345678 | shc”). This is the field that is used to derive the primary key of this table



specialty_source_value (string)

This is the kind of provider or specialty as it appears in the source data. This includes physician specialties such as internal medicine, emergency medicine, etc. and allied health professionals such as nurses, midwives, and pharmacists.



specialty_source_concept_id (int64)

The concept ID representing the source value, if available. This may be a custom concept (2,000,000,000 or higher) if the source value is not coded in an OMOP vocabulary. For now this is zero for all providers.



gender_source_value (string)

This is an integer representing provider’s sex as it appears in the source data. 1 for Female, 2 for Male



gender_source_concept_id (int64)

The concept ID representing the source value, if available. This may be a custom concept (2,000,000,000 or higher) if the source value is not coded in an OMOP vocabulary. For now this is zero for all providers.

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
RELATIONSHIP
The RELATIONSHIP table provides a reference list of all types of relationships that can be used to associate any two Concepts in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table, the respective reverse relationships, and their hierarchical characteristics. Note that Concepts representing relationships between the clinical facts in the FACT_RELATIONSHIP table are stored in the CONCEPT table and belong to the Relationship Domain.


relationship_id (string)

The type of relationship captured by the relationship record.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




relationship_name (string)

Relationship name.
Constraints:

Not Null




is_hierarchical (string)

Defines whether a relationship defines concepts into classes or hierarchies. Values are 1 for hierarchical relationship or 0 if not.
Constraints:

Not Null




defines_ancestry (string)

Defines whether a hierarchical relationship contributes to the concept_ancestor table. These are subsets of the hierarchical relationships. Valid values are 1 or 0.
Constraints:

Not Null




reverse_relationship_id (string)

The identifier for the relationship used to define the reverse relationship between two concepts.
Constraints:

Not Null




relationship_concept_id (int64)

A foreign key to the CONCEPT table for the unique relationship concept.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)
Not Null

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
SPECIMEN
The specimen table captures data on biological samples, or specimens, from a person. A specimen record indicates characteristics, such as date/time, specimen type, specimen source, and anatomic site of origin, in which the specimen was collected. This table is not currently populated in STARR OMOP.


specimen_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each specimen record.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the person table for each specimen record.



specimen_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of specimen for the specimen record.



specimen_type_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the provenance of the specimen record, such as it being a fact or observation from an EHR or survey.



specimen_date (DATE)

The date when the specimen was collected from the Person.



specimen_datetime (DATETIME)

The date and time when the specimen was collected from the Person.



quantity (FLOAT64)

Quantity of the specimen collected.



unit_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies representing the unit of measure for the specimen quantity.



anatomic_site_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies representing the anatomic site from which the specimen was obtained.



disease_status_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept identifier in the Standardized Vocabularies representing the disease status associated with the specimen.



specimen_source_id (STRING)

The identifier for the specimen in the source data.



specimen_source_value (STRING)

The source code for the specimen as it appears in the source data.



unit_source_value (STRING)

The source code for the unit of measure of the specimen quantity as it appears in the source data.



anatomic_site_source_value (STRING)

The source code for the anatomic site of the specimen as it appears in the source data.



disease_status_source_value (STRING)

The source code for the disease status associated with the specimen as it appears in the source data.

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
VISIT_DETAIL
The VISIT_DETAIL table is an optional table used to represents details of each record in the parent VISIT_OCCURRENCE table. A good example of this would be the movement between units in a hospital during an inpatient stay. For every record in the VISIT_OCCURRENCE table there may be 0 or more records in the VISIT_DETAIL table. The VISIT_DETAIL table is structurally very similar to VISIT_OCCURRENCE table and belongs to the visit domain.


visit_detail_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each visit detail record.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the person who is the subject of care during the visit.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('person')(person_id)




visit_detail_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the type of visit detail.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




visit_detail_start_date (date)

The date when the visit detail event started.



visit_detail_start_datetime (datetime)

The date and time when the visit detail event started.



visit_detail_end_date (date)

The date when the visit detail event ended.



visit_detail_end_datetime (datetime)

The date and time when the visit detail event ended.



visit_detail_type_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the provenance of the visit detail record. For STARR-OMOP, this is always ‘EHR Encounter Record’
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




provider_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the provider who is responsible for the visit detail event.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('provider')(provider_id)




care_site_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the care site where the visit detail event occurred.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('care_site')(care_site_id)




visit_detail_source_value (STRING)

The source value for the visit detail record.



visit_detail_source_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the source of the visit detail record. For STARR-OMOP, this is 0 for all records.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




admitted_from_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the location of the patient prior to the current visit detail. In STARR-OMOP, this is populated based on the prior department within a given encounter.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




admitted_from_source_value (STRING)

The source value for the location of the patient prior to the current visit detail.



discharged_to_source_value (STRING)

The source value for the location of the patient after the current visit detail.



discharged_to_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the predefined concept in the Standardized Vocabularies reflecting the location of the patient after the current visit detail. In STARR-OMOP, this is populated based on the subsequent department within a given encounter.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




preceding_visit_detail_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the visit detail record that immediately preceded the current visit detail record.



parent_visit_detail_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the visit detail record that is the parent of the current visit detail record. In STARR-OMOP, this is null for all records.



visit_occurrence_id (INT64)

A foreign key to the visit occurrence record that subsumes the visit detail record.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('visit_occurrence')(visit_occurrence_id)

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
VISIT_OCCURRENCE
This table contains Events where Persons engage with the healthcare system for a duration of time. They are often also called “Encounters”. Visits are defined by a configuration of circumstances under which they occur, such as (i) whether the patient comes to a healthcare institution, the other way around, or the interaction is remote, (ii) whether and what kind of trained medical staff is delivering the service during the Visit, and (iii) whether the Visit is transient or for a longer period involving a stay in bed.


visit_occurrence_id (INT64)

A unique identifier for each visit. This identifier is stable across STARR-OMOP releases.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




person_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the person in the person table who is the subject of the visit.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('person')(person_id)




visit_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the concept in the concept table representing the visit type (such as inpatient, outpatient, telehealth, etc.).
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




visit_start_date (DATE)

The date when the visit started.



visit_start_datetime (DATETIME)

The date and time when the visit started. If no time information is available, the time is set to 00:00:00.



visit_end_date (DATE)

The date when the visit ended. If no end date is available, the end date is assumed to be the same as the start date.



visit_end_datetime (DATETIME)

The date and time when the visit ended. If no end datetime is available, the end datetime is assumed to be the same as the start datetime. If no time information is available, the time is set to 00:00:00.



visit_type_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the concept in the concept table representing the provenance of the visit record. For STARR-OMOP, this is always ‘EHR encounter record’.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




provider_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the provider in the provider table who is the main care provider for the patient during the visit.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('provider')(provider_id)




care_site_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the care site in the care site table where the visit occurred.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('care_site')(care_site_id)




visit_source_value (STRING)

Descriptors for the visit type as they appear in the source data stored in a JSON. The source fields are encounter type, ADT (admission/discharge/transfer) patient class, hospital admission type, and appointment procedure (aka visit type). Note that not all fields are populated for all visits.



visit_source_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the concept in the concept table representing the visit type as it appears in the source data. In STARR-OMOP, this is set to 0 as we derive the final visit type concept ID from the source information via an algorithm.



admitted_from_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the standard concept in the concept table representing the admitting source.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




admitted_from_source_value (STRING)

The verbatim value for the admitting source as it appears in the source data.



discharged_to_concept_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the standard concept in the concept table representing the discharge destination.
Constraints:

Foreign Key → ref('concept')(concept_id)




discharged_to_source_value (STRING)

The verbatim value for the discharge destination as it appears in the source data.



preceding_visit_occurrence_id (INT64)

A foreign key identifier to the visit in the visit table that immediately preceded the current visit for that patient.

title: OMOP CDM v5.4 Data Model
text:
VOCABULARY
The VOCABULARY table includes a list of the Vocabularies integrated from various sources or created de novo in OMOP CDM. This reference table contains a single record for each Vocabulary and includes a descriptive name and other associated attributes for the Vocabulary. The primary purpose of the VOCABULARY table is to provide explicit information about specific vocabulary versions and the references to the sources from which they are asserted. Users can identify the version of a particular vocabulary used in the database, enabling consistency and reproducibility in data analysis.


vocabulary_id (string)

A unique identifier for each Vocabulary, such as ICD9CM, SNOMED, Visit.
Constraints:

Primary Key
Not Null




vocabulary_name (string)

The name describing the vocabulary, for example, International Classification of Diseases, Ninth Revision, Clinical Modification, Volume 1 and 2 (NCHS) etc.
Constraints:

Not Null




vocabulary_reference (string)

External reference to documentation or available download of the about the vocabulary.



vocabulary_version (string)

Version of the Vocabulary as indicated in the source.



vocabulary_concept_id (int64)

An identifier for the vocabulary that the VOCABULARY record belongs to. The vocabulary_concept_id is a foreign key to the CONCEPT table.
Constraints:

Not Null
